title: campanula macrorhiza var. sabatina
intro: Campanula macrorhiza var. sabatina, also known as Sabatina Bellflower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its bell-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Campanula macrorhiza var. sabatina include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce congestion, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory well-being and support the immune system.
